messages.content: 也许你可以找到Equal access to online information? Google’s suicide-prevention disparities may amplify a global digital divide这篇文献，并帮我介绍具体内容吗

这篇文献《Equal access to online information? Google’s suicide-prevention disparities may amplify a global digital divide》探讨了Google自杀预防搜索结果的差异，暗示了数字鸿沟的存在。研究表明，英语为母语的国家和地区的搜索结果具有更高质量的生命防范信息，而非英语使用者则找到的信息较少。本文强调了数字不平等的存在，尤其是对可能患有精神健康问题的群体而言，这种不平等如此关键。因此，社会应该更加重视如何逐步消除数字鸿沟。
可以帮我介绍一下这篇文献相关研究的主要背景吗

这篇文献主要是探讨数字鸿沟的问题，特别是在精神健康领域的案例。数字鸿沟是指由于社会、经济以及文化原因，导致某些人群无法访问或使用数字技术，因而在信息获取或社交方面会受到限制。该问题对于那些可能面临着心理健康问题的人来说，特别重要，因为他们可能会试图寻找相关信息和支持。此外，该文献还关注Google搜索引擎在搜索结果中呈现的信息质量，在这种情况下，是否存在差异对于在其搜索结果中寻找帮助的人们而言尤为重要。因此，研究者希望通过该研究，引起社会对于数字鸿沟问题的关注。
请说明文献涉及到的主要理论，需涵盖：理论的缘起，理论框架，主要的研究问题，涉及到的核心变量（或具体的研究对象）

此文献的理论基础是数字鸿沟理论。数字鸿沟理论最初是指由于信息和通信技术的普及而导致的信息数量和质量上的差异。在本研究中，数字鸿沟的定义扩展到了不同语言的人们之间的信息获取差异，尤其是在寻求精神健康信息的情况下。

该文献的理论框架基于先前在数字鸿沟领域的研究和文献回顾的基础之上构建。研究者通过分析Google在搜索结果中提供生命防范信息时对于不同语言和地区的用户提供的内容的质量差异，来探究数字鸿沟的存在是否会在心理健康领域中产生不公平的影响。 

该文献的主要研究问题是在Google的自杀预防搜索结果中，不同语言和地区的人们是否同等地获得了生命防范信息。由此产生的问题涉及到数字鸿沟、跨文化研究、精神健康问题以及社会公正性等方面。

该文献的核心变量是语言和地区，关注这些因素对于Google搜索结果及自杀预防信息提供的差异性，探究不同语言和地区的因素是否会产生数字不平等问题，并分析这种不平等情况如何对心理健康领域影响。
请你把这篇文献翻译成中文

Equal access to online information? Google’s suicide-prevention disparities may amplify a global digital divide

《在线信息的平等获取？谷歌自杀预防偏差可能会加剧全球数字鸿沟》

这篇论文探讨了Google在自杀预防领域搜索结果的差异，指出了数字鸿沟的存在。研究表明，使用英语的国家和地区搜索结果拥有更高质量的生命防范信息，而非英语使用者的信息则较少。文章重点强调了这种数字不平等的存在，并指出对于可能患有精神健康问题的人群而言，这种不平等尤其关键。因此，社会应该更加重视逐步消除数字鸿沟的问题。
请将这篇文献内容逐字逐句翻译成中文，可以先从前300字开始。

Equal access to online information? Google’s suicide-prevention disparities may amplify a global digital divide

在线信息的平等获取？谷歌自杀预防偏差可能会加剧全球数字鸿沟

Google’s search algorithms have long reflected our personal and societal biases.

谷歌的搜索算法长期以来一直反映了我们个人和社会的偏见。

If you typed a job title into the search box last year, for instance, men were more likely to be shown high-paying executive positions with “CEO” or “CFO” immediately following the job title. Women were more likely to see lower-paying service positions with “assistant” or “administrative” in the job title.

例如，如果你去年在搜索框中输入一个职位头衔，那么男性更有可能看到与“CEO”或“CFO”紧随头衔的高薪高管职位。女性更可能看到职位头衔中带有“助理”或“行政”的低薪服务职位。

Unfortunately, new research suggests that the biases in search algorithms extend to suicide prevention as well.

不幸的是，新的研究表明，搜索算法中的偏见也扩展到了自杀预防领域。

Suicide is a global public health crisis. According to the World Health Organization, close to 800,000 people die by suicide each year, and it is the second leading cause of death among young people aged 15-29 years old.

自杀是全球公共卫生危机。根据世界卫生组织的数据，每年有近80万人死于自杀，自杀在15-29岁年轻人中是第二大死因。

In response, several tech companies, including Facebook and Google, have rolled out suicide prevention tools to help people in distress.

对此，包括Facebook和Google在内的多家科技公司推出了自杀预防工具，以帮助处于困境中的人们。

These tools allow users to search for suicide prevention information and connect with support resources.

这些工具允许用户搜索自杀预防信息并连接支持资源。

However, a new study by researchers at Imperial College London suggests that the quality of these suicide prevention tools may vary based on the user’s location and language.

然而，伦敦帝国学院的研究人员开展的一项新研究表明，这些自杀预防工具的质量可能会根据用户的所在地和语言而有所不同。
下面我会给你发一些英文，请你帮我翻译成中文

Worldwide, people profit from equally accessible online health information via search engines. Therefore, equal access to health information is a global imperative. We studied one specific scenario, in which Google functions as a gatekeeper when people seek suiciderelated information using both helpful and harmful suicide-related search terms. To help prevent suicides, Google implemented a “suicide-prevention result” (SPR) at the very top of such search results. While this effort deserves credit, the present investigation compiled evidence that the SPR is not equally displayed to all users. Using a virtual agent-based testing methodology, a set of 3 studies in 11 countries found that the presentation of the SPR varies depending on where people search for suicide-related information. Language is a key factor explaining these differences. Google’s algorithms thereby contribute to a global digital divide in online health-information access with possibly lethal consequences. 
Higher and globally balanced display frequencies are desirable.

全球范围内，人们通过搜索引擎从平等且易于获取的在线健康信息中受益。因此，平等获得健康信息是全球必要的。我们研究了一个特定场景，在此场景下，当人们使用有益和有害的与自杀有关的搜索词时，谷歌起到了守门人的作用。为了帮助预防自杀，谷歌在这些搜索结果的顶部实施了一项“自杀预防结果”（SPR）服务。虽然这个努力值得称赞，但本次调查已经收集到证据表明，SPR并不会对所有用户同等展示。通过使用虚拟代理测试方法，我们进行了一系列的研究，在11个国家发现SPR的展示因搜索自杀相关信息的位置而异。语言是解释这些差异的关键因素。谷歌的算法因此对在线健康信息获取的全球数字鸿沟产生了贡献，可能会造成致命的后果。因此，更高和全球平衡的展示频率是我们所需要的。

Agent-based testing, big data, computational methods, digital divide, digital inequality, 
Google algorithm, Internet search engines, online information seeking, online search 
behavior, suicide prevention
代理基础测试，大数据，计算方法，数字鸿沟，数字不平等，谷歌算法，互联网搜索引擎，在线信息搜索，在线搜索行为，自杀预防。

Suicide is a major health problem causing approximately 800,000 annual deaths worldwide—that is one suicide every 40 seconds—and is the second leading cause of death 
among 15- to 29-year-olds (WHO, 2014, 2017). The social, emotional and economic 
consequences of suicides are dreadful. Importantly, the pivotal role of the media for suicides and suicide prevention is not questioned anymore by suicide prevention experts 
(see WHO, 2017). In fact, nonfictional and fictional media depictions of suicides grant 
access to both harmful and protective information (e.g. Till et al., 2017).
Google optimized its search-results page to provide better access to suicide prevention information (Google Official Blog, 2010, November). Consequently, Google 
implemented a so-called suicide-prevention result (SPR; see Figure 4 in the Appendix 
1 for a screenshot), which is prominently displayed at the top of its search results and 
depicts important online and offline help resources such as country-specific helpline 
telephone numbers, crisis chat rooms, and websites. For Google, establishing the SPR 
reflects their self-imposed corporate social responsibility to “do the right thing” 
(Alphabet Inc., 2017), including its web search service, which in turn potentially grants 
access to both helpful and harmful information about suicide (e.g. Gunn and Lester, 
2013; Thornton et al., 2017). For example, “doing the right thing” can mean to increase 
the display rates of the SPR by integrating social category information (and related trace 
evidence) into the algorithms, which can, however, cause outcome bias, or statistical 
discrimination (see Williams et al., 2018). Relatedly, local data-protection laws and 
Google’s corporate responsibility for data privacy can be in tension with more widely 
offering the SPR, further optimizing underlying algorithms by analyzing user data, and 
not jeopardizing the users’ trust in the platform (e.g. Dinev and Hart, 2005; Sundar and 
Marathe, 2010). Google’s SPR can be understood as a tailored awareness message that 
appears exactly when someone googles for a potentially harmful, suicide-related search 
term—similar to an intervention, that is presented to users who are deemed to be in an 
acute suicidal state based on their search terms. The SPR presents helpful, potentially 
protective information right in the moment when apparently needed (i.e. when users are 
looking for suicide-related terms), a beneficial temporal contiguity. Given the high 
number of Google users worldwide (Internet Live Stats, 2017), this has enormous 
potential for suicide prevention.
Shedding light on algorithmic decision-making, especially in socially relevant fields 
such as public health, is crucial in order to understand inequalities, biases, discrimination, and other undesired consequences of technology (Beer, 2017). Importantly, algorithms invisible to the public eye make it difficult to hold companies accountable for 
their algorithmic decisions (Brake, 2017; Janssen and Kuk, 2016). Hence, the present 
study contributes to the discussion on algorithm transparency and accountability as well 
as to technocratic or algorithmic governance in a broader sense (Janssen and Kuk, 2016; 
Just and Latzer, 2016).
Google does not disclose its algorithmic detection of acute suicidality. Only one previous study from Germany showed that Google’s SPR is displayed to a limited number 
of potentially vulnerable individuals: One-in-ten Google searches for helpful search 
terms (e.g. “overcoming suicidal thoughts”) and only one-in-four searches for harmful 
search terms (e.g. “best method for suicide”) triggered the display of the SPR (Haim 
et al., 2017).

自杀是一个重大的健康问题，全球每年有大约80万人死于自杀，即每40秒就有一起自杀事件发生，自杀是15-29岁人群中的第二大死因（世界卫生组织，2014，2017年）。自杀的社会、情感和经济后果是可怕的。值得注意的是，自杀预防专家不再质疑媒体对于自杀和自杀预防的关键作用（见世界卫生组织，2017年）。事实上，自杀的非虚构和虚构媒体描写都提供了有害和有保护的信息（例如Till等人，2017年）。

谷歌将其搜索结果页面进行了优化，以便更好地访问自杀预防信息（谷歌官方博客，2010年11月）。因此，谷歌实施了一个名为自杀预防结果（SPR）的服务（请参见附录1中图4的截屏），该服务在搜索结果的顶部突出显示，并显示重要的在线和离线援助资源，例如特定国家的热线电话，危机聊天室和网站。对于谷歌来说，建立SPR反映了其自我强加的企业社会责任，要“做正确的事情”（Alphabet Inc.，2017年），包括其网络搜索服务，这反过来可能获取有关自杀的有益和有害信息（例如Gunn和Lester，2013年；Thornton等人，2017年）。

可以将谷歌的SPR理解为一条定制的意识信息，当某人使用与自杀相关的搜素词进行谷歌搜索时，它就会出现-类似于在搜索词汇暴露问题的人群中出现的干预措施。SPR在用户需要其帮助的瞬间提供有用的、潜在的保护信息（即当用户正在寻找与自杀相关的词汇时）。考虑到全球谷歌用户的数量之
